关于.text:
We uses cookies to ensure you get the best experience! By continuing to use our site, you agree to our cookie policy. Read our cookie policy.
Agree
Select Main Body Text
:::
ABOUT
Award Introduction
First established in Taiwan in 1981, the Golden Pin Design Award (GPDA) is now one of the most representative and influential design awards in the global Hua-ren market. Since 2014, GPDA opened its application to designs of the world, becoming an international award with the mission of elevating domestic design vision as well as fostering international design exchanges. In 2015, GPDA further evolved into an award brand, expanding into three separate awards that together give stage to the comprehensive design ecosystem: the Golden Pin Design Award for professional design teams, the Golden Pin Concept Design Award for emerging designers, and the Young Pin Design Award for the graduating design students.

The GPDA brand not only recognizes outstanding designs, more importantly we strive to encourage more industry adaption of design thinking, process, and research & development, through professional recognition. We believe that by harnessing the power of design to enhance brand value, the award serves as a quality certification for the market and consumers, promoting greater public awareness of the importance of design and aesthetics, and ultimately contributing to a better quality of life for all.

1981-2001 GD-Mark

We established a system to reward outstanding product design with the goal of promoting a nation-wide emphasis on “good product design,” and implemented a professional selection process to encourage manufacturers to develop and design new products.

2002-2008 GD-Mark

In an unprecedented move, when we decided to retool the name, logo, and slogan of the outstanding product design award, we opened up the selection process to involve the public in the movement to recognize and appreciate good product design.

2005-2008 The National Design Award

Since 2005, participants who were granted the right to use GD-Mark have been able to submit entries to the National Design Award.

2009-2013 Golden Pin Design Award

A simple, easy-to-understand and easy-to-remember title with a global orientation—via an open name selection process followed by an open voting and discussions by a panel of experts in a campaign to turn the Golden Pin into a world-class design award.

2014 Golden Pin Design Award

Elevated the stature of Chinese design. During the first year that our call for projects included those from abroad, we emphasized design quality and recognized outstanding performance among independent designers and professionals in the design industry, providing an opportunity for the global market to experience their design strengths.

Golden Pin Design Award ｜ The Hall of Honor for Professional Designs

The Golden Pin Design Award has become a prestigious stage for professional designers and enterprises striving for excellence. Each year, an international jury body selects outstanding works from commercially available products and completed spatial or integrated design projects to receive the "Golden Pin Design Mark," recognizing their design quality and value. Among them, only a select few are awarded the highest honors—the annual "Best Design Award" or the "Special Award"—to highlight their exceptional performance and groundbreaking achievements.

 

Golden Pin Concept Design Award ｜ Innovative Proposals for the Future

The Golden Pin Concept Design Award is dedicated to design ideas that have yet to be realized. It encourages students, designers, and startups to boldly explore forward-thinking ideas that may lead the future. Over the years, countless emerging talents have launched from this platform, gradually advancing their concepts into the market and society. To further support promising entries, three winners of the “Best Design of the Year” are selected annually and granted prize money to help bring their “concepts to life.”

 

Young Designers Award ｜ The Dream Stage for Emerging Designers

Originally established in 2001 as the “Young Designers Award,” the award was formally incorporated into the Golden Pin Design Awards brand system in 2015. As the sole official award of the Young Designers’ Exhibition (YODEX) —Taiwan’s largest annual showcase of graduating design talent—the Young Pin Design Award not only provides a creative platform for the next generation but also serves as an important launchpad for many young designers as they step into their professional careers.

Golden Pin Design Award

Design entry must be already commercialized on the market. The vendor applicant must be a business entity or design team. 

Golden Pin Concept
Design Award

Entries are limited to works that will not be manufactured or sold on the market before December 31, 2021. Projects commissioned by a client and projects in active development are ineligible.

Golden Pin Design Award Registration Guidelines
 Winner Benefits
 Award Overview
 Registration Guidelines
Certificates and Trophies
Use of Mark
Award Ceremony
Inclusion on Official Website
Best Design of the Year Special Publication
Annual Exhibition
Business Opportunities
Media Exposure
Design Exchange
Jury Feedback
Certificates and Trophies

Every winning entry will be awarded a certificate. The winner of the Best Design of the Year will also receive a specially crafted trophy, symbolizing the highest honor.

 




Introduction to the Trophy Design >

 

Use of Mark

Winners are granted the right to use the Golden Pin Mark, which can be utilized for product packaging, sales platforms, various advertisements, or social media to highlight the award-winning achievement of the product or work.

 




Award Ceremony

During the grand award ceremony, the winner of the Best Design of the Year is ceremoniously announced, marking a moment of triumph for the designer. The Design Night event on that evening also provides an opportunity for professionals in the global design and creative industries to network and exchange ideas.

Previous
Next
Inclusion on Official Website

Winning entries will be displayed on the official Golden Pin Design Award website along with their descriptions as we as pertinent information about designers, companies, and more, allowing exceptional designs to gain greater visibility and recognition.

Best Design of the Year Special Publication

Entries honored with the Best Design of the Year will be prominently featured in a dedicated publication with specially allocated pages.

Previous
Next
Annual Exhibition

Winning entries will have the opportunity to be chosen for display at the annual “Golden Pin Design Award Exhibition” of that year, allowing them to share their creative essence with both design professionals and the public.

Previous
Next
Business Opportunities

Award-winning products will have the opportunity to be showcased in the “Design Pin” for promotion and sales, available through online and physical store channels where award-winning designs from the Golden Pin Design Award and other international accolades are featured and available for purchase. This not only enhances brand image but also creates more business opportunities.

Additionally, the Golden Pin Design Award collaborates with the Japan Institute of Design Promotion (JDP), the organizer of the Good Design Award, to establish a dedicated Golden Pin section at the Good Design Store Tokyo by NOHARA. Winning products from previous years will have the chance to be showcased here, further expanding opportunities for international market penetration and global exposure.

The Golden Pin’s business matching platform enables participating products to engage in online and offline negotiations with domestic and international B2B buyers across different fields, expanding market opportunities.

Media Exposure

Each year, the Golden Pin Design Award collaborates with numerous prominent Taiwanese and international media outlets as official media partners. Through coverage of award-winning works and interviews with designers, these media partners play a vital role in promoting outstanding designs.



Design Exchange

The Golden Pin Design Award organizes a myriad of design exchange activities every year, among them the Design Perspectives x Golden Pin Salon. We invites juries and award-winning designers to delve into contemporary design trends and collectively explore future directions. Such endeavors foster the development and innovation of both the design industry and cultures.

Since 2015, the Golden Pin Salon has spanned over 20 cities across Asia, including Taipei, Shanghai, Chengdu, Shenzhen, Hong Kong, Macau, Kuala Lumpur and Singapore. It has showcased the works of nearly a hundred designers and creative professionals from various fields, drawing thousands of design enthusiasts and garnering widespread acclaim.

Jury Feedback

Entries receive feedback from the preliminary selection, where jurors evaluate and rate each entry individually based on the selection criteria, allowing participants to better understand how their product compares to others.

Harudot Chonburi by Nana Coffee Roasters
MARK WINNER | BEST DESIGN
Category
Spatial Design
SubCategory
Dining space
Applicant Company
IDIN Architects / Thailand
Manufacturer / Business Owner
Pongpanot Tanomboon / Thailand
Design Company
IDIN Architects / Thailand

Harudot: Where nature brews a new beginning. Here, "Haru" whispers fresh beginnings, while "Dot" marks the start of something special. The gables are pulled apart to allow for trees to grow through the building. Inside, curving Barrisol ceilings echo the natural rhythms, blurring the lines between inside and out. The exterior is clad in blackened wood to exude simplicity while the interior radiates an unforeseen contrast through dynamic curves and a warm tone wood finishing.


Jury's Evaluation

A unique design language was utilized to create harmony between nature and architecture for the Harudot coffee shop, reflecting the poetic dialogue between people and the environment while elegantly conveying the brand spirit. From the masterful use of space in the layout to the clever connection of internal and external spaces, every detail demonstrates the precise command of material, light, shadow, and ambience.
